title: Scaling and evaluating sparse autoencoders
reason: Evaluates cybersecurity capabilities of LM agents on vulnerability identification and exploit execution tasks.

title: Spider 2.0: Evaluating Language Models on Real-World Enterprise Text-to-SQL Workflows
reason: Evaluates LLM agents on complex real-world SQL workflows, directly testing code agent capabilities for enterprise programming tasks.

title: RefactorBench: Evaluating Stateful Reasoning in Language Agents Through Code
reason: Benchmark for stateful LM agents on complex, multi-file code refactoring tasks; directly evaluates reasoning for code.

title: To Code or Not To Code? Exploring Impact of Code in Pre-training
reason: Systematically analyzes impact of code data in pre-training on downstream reasoning, linking code quality to NL reasoning gains.

title: Language Models Learn to Mislead Humans via RLHF
reason: Studies RLHF's effect on code task (APPS) correctness & human evaluation, relevant to LLM reliability for programming.

title: Benchmarking Agentic Workflow Generation
reason: Benchmarks LLM agent workflow generation for complex planning, evaluating graph-structured reasoning.

title: ConvCodeWorld: Benchmarking Conversational Code Generation in Reproducible Feedback Environments
reason: Benchmarks multi-turn code generation with execution/compilation feedback, relevant to LLM reliability for code correctness.

title: Autonomous Evaluation of LLMs for Truth Maintenance and Reasoning Tasks
reason: Autoformalization benchmark for LLM reasoning with clear correctness criteria; relates to structured reasoning & verification.

title: AndroidWorld: A Dynamic Benchmarking Environment for Autonomous Agents
reason: AndroidWorld benchmark for autonomous agents controlling computers; tests LLM agents on programmatic mobile app tasks.

title: BALROG: Benchmarking Agentic LLM and VLM Reasoning On Games
reason: Benchmarks LLM/VLM agentic reasoning in complex, dynamic environments, relevant to evaluating code agents.

title: CS-Bench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Large Language Models towards Computer Science Mastery
reason: Benchmark for LLMs on CS knowledge; includes code generation/reasoning evaluation, relevant for reliability assessment.

title: The Rise and Down of Babel Tower: Investigating the Evolution Process of Multilingual Code Large Language Model
reason: Studies multilingual code LLM knowledge evolution; relevant for understanding code reasoning capabilities.

title: BadRobot: Jailbreaking Embodied LLM Agents in the Physical World
reason: Jailbreaking embodied LLM agents; connects agent safety risks to physical code execution.

title: Improved Techniques for Optimization-Based Jailbreaking on Large Language Models
reason: Studies jailbreaking as a reliability/safety failure mode for LLMs; relates to adversarial robustness of code-generation systems.

title: LiveCodeBench: Holistic and Contamination Free Evaluation of Large Language Models for Code
reason: Benchmark for holistic code LLM evaluation, including self-repair and test output prediction, relevant to reliability testing.

title: InsightBench: Evaluating Business Analytics Agents Through Multi-Step Insight Generation
reason: Evaluates LLM agents for end-to-end data analytics involving code generation and insight summarization.

title: Dissecting Adversarial Robustness of Multimodal LM Agents
reason: Evaluates robustness of LM agents in a structured environment, relevant to reliability of code-generating agents.

title: GraphArena: Evaluating and Exploring Large Language Models on Graph Computation
reason: Evaluates LLMs on graph algorithms, explores reliability methods like chain-of-thought and code writing for correctness.
